seoTitle: Pleasure Point vs Point Clare — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pleasure Point and Point Clare. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pleasure Point vs Point Clare: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pleasure Point and Point Clare.
heading: Pleasure Point vs Point Clare — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pleasure Point, New South Wales and Point Clare, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Clare has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Pleasure Point (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pleasure Point or Point Clare safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pleasure Point has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Point Clare scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pleasure Point scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Point Clare scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pleasure Point or Point Clare?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Clare has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
